basic_details.job_title: Indian Air Force MTS Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for Multi-Tasking Staff Posts
basic_details.short_information: Daily Current Affairs 23 June 2026: Download Free PDF Now

Desirable: One year of experience as a Watchman, Lascar, Gestetner Operator, or Gardener from any recognized Organization or Institute. | Meeting only the essential qualification does not guarantee a call for the written test. | Applicants must be eligible Indian citizens as per the provisions applicable to Central Government recruitment.

This is the entry-level pay band for Group C civilian employees under the Government of India, and selected candidates will be entitled to all applicable central government allowances and benefits under relevant service rules. | Note: Selected candidates will be subject to 'All India Service Liability', meaning they can be posted to any location across India as required by the Air Force. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: The IAF MTS recruitment follows a clearly defined selection process. Knowing each stage in advance helps candidates prepare effectively and avoid being eliminated at any step.
All applications received will be examined for age limit compliance
and certificates. Only eligible candidates will be shortlisted for the written test.
Eligible shortlisted candidates will be called for a written test. The selection of candidates will be based purely on performance in the written test — no interview is conducted.
Written Test Syllabus covers four areas:
General Intelligence and Reasoning Numerical Aptitude General English General Awareness
The question-cum-answer paper will be available in both English and Hindi.
Shortlisted candidates after the written test must bring original certificates and copies of the annexure attached with the application at the time of document verification.
Important: Candidates are required to bring original certificates of SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/ESM
and experience at every stage of recruitment. The crucial date for determining validity of documents is on or before the last date of receipt of application.
how_to_apply: The Indian Air Force MTS 2026 recruitment follows an offline application process. There is no online application portal for this vacancy. Here is a complete guide to submitting your application correctly.
Before filling the form, gather the following:
Caste/Community Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) - issued by competent civil authority (self-attested)
EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026-27 (or FY 2025-26 if closing date falls before March 31, 2026 - issued as per DoPT OM No. 36039/1/2019-Estt(Res.) dated January 31, 2019)
PwBD Certificate (as per DoPT OM No. 29-6/2019-DD-III dated August 10, 2022, if applicable)
Two recent passport-size photographs (same as affixed on the application form)
Self-addressed envelope with Rs. 10/- stamp (address must clearly mention "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF ______ AND CATEGORY ______")
Use the Proforma (Annexure-I) provided in the advertisement
Type or write the application in English or Hindi
Affix a recent passport-size photograph (self-attested) on the application form
Attach one self-attested photograph for the Acknowledgement Card as well
important_instructions: Missing any of these instructions could lead to rejection of your application. Read these carefully before sending your form.

Applications received before the publication date of this notification will be rejected Applications received after the closing date will not be entertained No TA/DA will be paid for attending the written test The examination centre will be intimated to the candidate in due course and may differ from the station to which the application was sent Pre-scrutiny of applications will be done by the unit before calling candidates for the written test The Air Force Station has the right to reject any application without assigning any reason Submission of false information at any stage will result in termination without notice Selected candidates will be subject to All India Service Liability

content_sections.heading: IAF MTS Recruitment 2026 - Overview
content_sections.text: Daily Current Affairs 23 June 2026: Download Free PDF Now

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Details - Category-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: Understanding the reservation structure is critical before applying, as candidates from reserved categories receive both age relaxation and reserved seats. Here is the complete category-wise vacancy distribution for the 6 MTS posts at IAF Bangalore. The vacancy notice specifies the following category-wise distribution under the HQ Maintenance Command, IAF: Key: UR - Unreserved (General) OBC - Other Backward Classes SC - Scheduled Castes ST - Scheduled Tribes EWS - Economically Weaker Section PwBD - Persons with Benchmark Disabilities ESM - Ex-Servicemen Note: The number of vacancies is subject to change. The Air Officer Commanding / Station Commander / Commanding Officer of the concerned Air Force Station reserves the right to alter vacancy numbers and reservation status at any time before selection.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria - Who Can Apply
content_sections.text: The general age requirement for the MTS post is 18 to 25 years , with the last date of receipt of application being the cut-off date for age calculation. Age Relaxation Available: Important: UR/OBC candidates applying against unreserved posts are not entitled to any age relaxation on account of experience. The post of MTS requires the following qualifications: Essential: Matriculation (Class 10 pass) or equivalent qualification from a recognized University or Board. Desirable: One year of experience as a Watchman, Lascar, Gestetner Operator, or Gardener from any recognized Organization or Institute. Meeting only the essential qualification does not guarantee a call for the written test. Applicants must be eligible Indian citizens as per the provisions applicable to Central Government recruitment.

rows.age_relaxation: Deduction of actual military service + 3 years (if resultant age does not exceed max by more than 3 years)

content_sections.heading: Pay Scale and Benefits
content_sections.text: One of the most important factors candidates consider before applying is the salary. Here is what the IAF MTS 2026 notification states about the pay structure. The MTS post carries a pay scale of Level-1 as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) Pay Matrix . This is the entry-level pay band for Group C civilian employees under the Government of India, and selected candidates will be entitled to all applicable central government allowances and benefits under relevant service rules. Note: Selected candidates will be subject to 'All India Service Liability', meaning they can be posted to any location across India as required by the Air Force. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: Selection Process - How Candidates Are Chosen
content_sections.text: The IAF MTS recruitment follows a clearly defined selection process. Knowing each stage in advance helps candidates prepare effectively and avoid being eliminated at any step. All applications received will be examined for age limit compliance, minimum educational qualification, required documents, and certificates. Only eligible candidates will be shortlisted for the written test. Eligible shortlisted candidates will be called for a written test . The selection of candidates will be based purely on performance in the written test — no interview is conducted. Written Test Syllabus covers four areas: General Intelligence and Reasoning Numerical Aptitude General English General Awareness The question-cum-answer paper will be available in both English and Hindi. Shortlisted candidates after the written test must bring original certificates and copies of the annexure attached with the application at the time of document verification. Important: Candidates are required to bring original certificates of SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/ESM, educational qualifications, technical qualifications, and experience at every stage of recruitment. The crucial date for determining validity of documents is on or before the last date of receipt of application.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply - Step-by-Step Application Guide
content_sections.text: The Indian Air Force MTS 2026 recruitment follows an offline application process . There is no online application portal for this vacancy. Here is a complete guide to submitting your application correctly. Before filling the form, gather the following: Proof of age (Birth Certificate, self-attested) Educational qualification certificates (self-attested) Caste/Community Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) - issued by competent civil authority (self-attested) EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026-27 (or FY 2025-26 if closing date falls before March 31, 2026 - issued as per DoPT OM No. 36039/1/2019-Estt(Res.) dated January 31, 2019) PwBD Certificate (as per DoPT OM No. 29-6/2019-DD-III dated August 10, 2022, if applicable) Ex-Serviceman Discharge Certificate Technical Training/Experience Certificate Domicile Certificate Employment Exchange Registration Card (if registered) Two recent passport-size photographs (same as affixed on the application form) Self-addressed envelope with Rs. 10/- stamp (address must clearly mention "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF ______ AND CATEGORY ______")

content_sections.heading: Where to Send the Application
content_sections.text: Applications must be sent by post to: Commandant, IAM, IAF, Vimanapura PO, Bangalore - 560017 OBC candidates must submit a certificate confirming they do not belong to the creamy layer of OBC, in accordance with the latest Central List rules. Only OBC candidates whose castes are listed under the Central OBC list are eligible for appointment to Central Services.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions to Remember
content_sections.text: Missing any of these instructions could lead to rejection of your application. Read these carefully before sending your form. Applications received before the publication date of this notification will be rejected Applications received after the closing date will not be entertained No TA/DA will be paid for attending the written test The examination centre will be intimated to the candidate in due course and may differ from the station to which the application was sent Pre-scrutiny of applications will be done by the unit before calling candidates for the written test The Air Force Station has the right to reject any application without assigning any reason Submission of false information at any stage will result in termination without notice Selected candidates will be subject to All India Service Liability

faq.question: Q1. How many MTS vacancies are there in Indian Air Force 2026?
faq.answer: The Indian Air Force has announced 6 MTS (Multi-Tasking Staff) vacancies at HQ Maintenance Command, Bangalore under Advertisement No. 02/2026. The category-wise distribution is: UR-3, OBC-2, EWS-1.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for IAF MTS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date is 30 days from the publication date of the advertisement in Employment News, which was June 27, 2026. This puts the deadline at approximately July 27, 2026. Applications must physically reach the unit by this date.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification for IAF MTS post 2026?
faq.answer: The essential qualification is Matriculation (Class 10 pass) or equivalent from a recognized University or Board. Desirably, one year of experience as a Watchman, Lascar, Gestetner Operator, or Gardener is preferred but not mandatory.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for IAF Group C MTS 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 25 years as on the last date of receipt of applications. Age relaxation is available for OBC (3 years), SC/ST (5 years), PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and Departmental Employees as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an interview for IAF MTS recruitment?
faq.answer: No. The selection for IAF MTS 2026 is based purely on the written test. There is no interview. Candidates must perform well in the written test covering General Intelligence, Numerical Aptitude, General English, and General Awareness.

faq.question: Q6. Can I apply online for IAF MTS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. This recruitment requires an offline application by post. There is no online application mode. Candidates must fill the Annexure-I proforma, attach required documents, and send it by post to Commandant, IAM, IAF, Vimanapura PO, Bangalore – 560017.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale for IAF MTS (Group C) post?
faq.answer: The IAF MTS post carries Level-1 pay as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) Pay Matrix. In addition to the basic pay, central government allowances such as DA, HRA, and transport allowance are applicable.

faq.question: Q8. Where is the posting location for IAF MTS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The posting location is HQ Maintenance Command, IAF, Vimanapura PO, Bangalore – 560017. However, since selected candidates are subject to All India Service Liability, they may be posted anywhere in India as per IAF requirements.

faq.question: Q9. What documents are needed for IAF MTS application 2026?
faq.answer: Key documents include: self-attested copies of Class 10 marksheet/certificate, age proof (Birth Certificate), caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), EWS certificate, PwBD certificate if applicable, Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate if applicable, two passport-size photographs, and a self-addressed envelope with Rs. 10/- stamp.

faq.question: Q10. Will TA/DA be provided for attending the IAF MTS written test?
faq.answer: No. The official notification clearly states that no TA/DA will be paid to candidates for attending the written test. Candidates must arrange their own travel and accommodation.
